title: Intro D-ledger residuals post-D43: D31/D32/D35 pose drift, D37/D39 static-shot camera seed, D38 duration (M1.5)

evidence.note: Initial first-poll-aligned stock-ares/native capture on 2026-07-22: D36 gunhand had zero divergences and its last waiver was removed; it measured D31 at 42/max 74.89u, D32 at 3 records, D35 across 13 fields (cam_pos[0] max 38.0043u; cam_floor[1] max 440.85u), and D41 at 108/max 1.0 frame. The follow-up evidence records why the first-poll D31/D35 counts and broad D41 count were superseded rather than silently discarded.

D41 resolution, 2026-07-22: a fresh native menu/direct comparison is exact across 1075 camera/setup/numeric-animation records, refuting the original menu-path phase diagnosis. Ares emits multiple controller polls per authored timer, so per-mode alignment now selects the settled last record (unit-gated); exact timer zero is excluded as the audited asynchronous DMA boundary. Stress captures then showed why no simulation change is valid: retail first samples the same 0.75 idle frame at timer 100 in one run and 102 in another, depending on its three-tick/controller-poll lattice. A +0.25 native seed matched the first and worsened the second, so it was removed before landing. The camera-independent Bond oracle event-aligns only the idle identity boundary within one retail batch and keeps numeric idle frame narrowly D41-waived pending a game-tick stock hook; phase-3 identity/metadata/coverage remain event-relative and strict. Repeated natural-seed captures also correct the earlier D36 conclusion: gunhand can be the opposite mirrored choice (103 stock/native records; 274 menu/direct) because wider boot histories reach randomGetNext() & 1 at different PRNG phases, so that field is narrowly owned by FID-0063. Settled matched-camera evidence leaves D31 at 1/max 0.26u, D32 at one duration verdict/delta 2 records, and D35 live across 10 camera fields (cam_pos[0] max 0.91u; cam_floor[1] max 440.85u).
